sections.title: Purposes of sentencing
sections.content: The purposes of sentencing are: to hold the offender accountable, to denounce the conduct, to deter the offender or others, to protect the community, to assist rehabilitation.

sections.title: Principles of sentencing
sections.content: The court must take into account: the gravity of the offence, the degree of culpability, the personal circumstances of the offender, and the extent of harm caused.

sections.title: Aggravating factors
sections.content: Aggravating factors include: vulnerability of victim, breach of trust, premeditation, group offending, hate motivation.

sections.title: Mitigating factors
sections.content: Mitigating factors include: remorse, guilty plea, youth, previous good character, cooperation with authorities.
